rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unsafe copy at program.c:13 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained that argv data can exceed the 256-byte destination, causing an AddressSanitizer stack-buffer-overflow. They explicitly described attacker-controlled input overflowing the stack buffer.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed rejecting overlong input (strlen(argv[1]) >= 256) before copying and then performing the copy, which keeps the destination null-terminated. This replaces the unbounded behavior with a length-checked copy sized to the buffer.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing raw, unbounded string handling by using bounded APIs or pointer+length patterns and centralizing validation before copies. This addresses the root cause by enforcing capacity-aware string operations throughout.
